notes: v1.14.6 — globe: removed the lat/lon graticule, replaced the flat body with a lit 3D ocean sphere. John rejected the v1.14.5 graticule (dotted lat/lon grid read too technical-diagram for the premium-space aesthetic). EDIT 1 deleted the entire graticule block — the 732-point grid no longer appends to staticData and all GRAT_ symbols are gone (border IIFE now leads straight into the arc-loop vars). EDIT 2 rebuilt the landing-globe body inside drawUnderlay() from a flat concentric gradient into a lit 3D ocean sphere = 3 stacked canvas-2D fills: (1) dark-ocean ball with limb darkening (rgba(12,22,42)->(3,6,14)) for curvature; (2) directional light from an upper-left sun point (LX/LY = CX/CY - 0.38R; radial 0.05R->1.35R) so the near limb is lit and the far lower-right limb falls into shadow = terminator; (3) a contained specular sheen/glint at the sun point (radial 0->0.55R). The old thin blue inner rim was dropped on purpose (dark limb = roundness); the existing halo (r 0.98-1.04, drawn just before the body) still supplies the outer atmosphere edge. drawUnderlay runs on resize only (single call site, NOT the rAF frame loop), so the three extra fills are one-shot and free. Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts + sw.js, block-0 CSS braces 2736/2736 (no CSS change — both edits are JS), CRLF preserved (0 lone LF), four stamps lockstep v1.14.6, sw.js cache-bumped, no GRAT_ symbols remain, git diff surgical. Adversarially verified (4-lens workflow). NOT runtime-tested — canvas/WebGL can't render in the sandbox; John's device watch: (1) empty/Pacific face reads as a lit 3D ocean sphere (bright upper-left, shadowed lower-right limb, faint glint) not a flat disc, (2) city lights/SPECTRUM still pop over the deep-navy base, (3) if the dark limb reads too hard the halo alpha stops (0.028/0.058) brighten the rim, (4) sun direction = upper-left via LX/LY signs / 0.38 offset. WARNING: standing repo-only fixes — v1.13.8 (#boot full-bleed) and v1.14.4 (storage-full recovery) still lack a counterpart in the external/web-Claude build base; re-apply on the next import or they drop.
